description.p: BedrockOnLinux installs and runs the Windows (GDK) edition of Minecraft Bedrock on Linux. One app does everything automatically:

ul.li: downloads the Minecraft version you pick;
applies the binary patches the game needs to start and to join online Bedrock servers;
fixes curl/SSL, then launches the game.

description.p: Sign in, open Play • Servers, and join native Bedrock servers (Hive, CubeCraft, …) or crossplay/Geyser servers. No game files are shipped; you supply your own — you must own Minecraft.

screenshot.caption: The launcher: pick a version, sign in, play

description.p: Makes sure the graphics-engine fix from 1.2.8 actually reaches you: updating the launcher and pressing Play now updates the game engine automatically when a newer one has shipped. Before, the engine only updated if you re-ran Install/Update by hand, so the fix for the ESC/menu freeze silently missed people who just pressed Play — if it still froze for you on 1.2.8, this pulls the fixed engine in on the next launch.
Fixes the game freezing when you press ESC / back, pause, or dismiss an in-game dialog — mostly on newer graphics cards and drivers.

ul.li: updates the bundled graphics engine (DXVK and vkd3d-proton) to 3.0.1; the older version couldn't build one of Minecraft's draw commands and fell back to a path that hangs on recent GPUs, which is what caused the freeze on ESC / back / pausing / closing a popup;
after updating, launch once to download the new engine.

description.p: Fixes the game breaking when Advanced Diagnostics is on, and adds a clearer warning when your GPU isn't being used:

ul.li: if the game falls back to software rendering (your GPU's Vulkan driver isn't active), the launcher now tells you — that is what makes the Play screen render black; a reboot or driver reinstall fixes it.

description.p: Fixes online sign-in and two crashes:

ul.li: adds an optional setting to keep the mouse cursor inside the window, so it no longer slips outside the game in windowed mode.

description.p: Fixes a false "reinstall the game engine" warning that could appear after closing the game even when everything had worked: the post-game check was matching an unrelated, harmless system message. It now trusts the engine's own sign-in markers, so it no longer cries wolf on a perfectly good session.
A refreshed, modern interface and a fix for launch crashes on some systems:

ul.li: a new setting to turn off the in-game Servers-tab unlock: on a few systems forcing it makes the game freeze or crash a few seconds after launch, and turning it off lets the game run (Steam Deck, CachyOS and some others);
the version list and downloads are more reliable, and downloads now resume instead of failing on a flaky connection.

description.p: Fixes the AppImage hanging at "Preparing" with an empty version list on some Linux distributions. It now bundles its own certificate store, so it can always reach the download servers over HTTPS no matter where the system keeps its certificates — the version list, the game engine download and Microsoft sign-in all work out of the box. Completes the self-contained AppImage from 1.1.5.

The AppImage is now fully self-contained: it bundles its own Python and the Tk GUI toolkit, so it runs on any Linux out of the box with nothing to install first. This fixes the "tkinter is not installed" error on systems that ship Python without the Tk module. The Flatpak was already self-contained and the Debian package pulls those pieces in automatically; the portable .pyz still uses the system Python.

Fixes the launch crash on recent Minecraft builds (1.26.30+) the proper way, so the in-game Servers tab is unlocked everywhere instead of being greyed out on those builds. The "is signed in with Microsoft" patch now guards against the account object the game has not finished setting up yet: it keeps the flag where the object exists and safely does nothing where it is missing, so the game starts on every system (Steam Deck, CachyOS and others) and still shows the server list. Delivered by the engine auto-update.

Fixes a crash a few seconds after launch on recent Minecraft builds (1.26.30+): a binary patch was forcing an "is signed in with Microsoft" flag that those builds trust to access an account object that does not exist under Wine, causing a null dereference. The patch is now only applied on builds where it is safe; newer builds run normally (with a greyed Servers tab) instead of crashing. The fixed engine is delivered by the engine auto-update.

The bundled game engine now auto-updates: when a newer WineGDK engine is published, the launcher re-downloads it on the next launch — the same way the launcher updates itself — so the in-game sign-in and server-join fixes reach everyone, not just fresh installs.

ul.li: the version picker now defaults to the version you last played (or the latest) instead of resetting to a different one;
the in-game sign-in and native-server-join binary patches now track the game across the 1.26.x updates.

description.p: Internal refactor into a Python package, with fixes for the open bug reports:

ul.li: fixes a startup crash and a spurious Steam launch caused by Proton's VR client;
fixes the Flatpak failing to start under a pure Wayland session;
makes Microsoft sign-in reliable by ensuring the required Python dependency is present on every install.

description.p: Flatpak packaging, plus two Wayland input fixes:

ul.li: opt-in gamescope (BOL_GAMESCOPE=1) so the in-game mouse works on Wayland sessions, where XWayland otherwise drops it;
the opt-in winewayland backend now falls back to XWayland automatically when it cannot open a window, so the game always starts.

description.p: Fixes for the opt-in winewayland backend and safer prefix repair.
